mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sudden surge in retail sales exceeding 5% year-over-year indicates overheating consumer demand, prompting near-term profit-taking and negative short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS crosses above 5% YoY growth threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in greater than 65% of triggered instances, or signal loses predictive power when CPIAUCSL remains below 230.

mechanism_narrative: Tight labor markets combined with rising payroll employment signal wage inflation pressures that force central banks to tighten policy, leading to negative equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or IC drops below zero in walk-forward tests over consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints that erode corporate earnings growth, causing negative quarterly equity returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not correlate with negative 60-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52% across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and equity risk appetite, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows, or earnings revisions do not follow GDP prints.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation exceeds 5% annually, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, triggering immediate negative short-term equity returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above threshold yields positive 5-day returns in greater than 60% of cases, or market ignores inflation prints due to liquidity dominance.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based domestic demand strength that supports corporate revenue growth and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint demand signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when supply chain bottlenecks constrain production, or IC remains negative over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate during a late-cycle expansion signals economic deceleration that reduces earnings growth expectations, driving negative monthly equity returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE increases are associated with positive 20-day returns in greater than 55% of instances, or signal reverses direction during recessionary periods.
